Mother's Day is supposed to be about gratitude.
[Pledge your support](https://substack..uzxjKRuhTFbvibttya37LEyp_WkDnWn0faA3w8pv64M?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=197052255)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=197052255&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk3MDUyMjU1LCJpYXQiOjE3Nzg0MzMxMjYsImV4cCI6MTc4MTAyNTEyNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.6jqhTP_SVjHo7Qqoe1Y4qwx93jLo8Z6edLnMwxcbKX4)
It is supposed to be about honoring the women who raise families, hold communities together, care for aging parents, and increasingly serve on the front lines of America's healthcare system.
_**But this year, many mothers in Connecticut are exhausted.**_
Not simply from parenting.
From navigating a system that feels increasingly impersonal, bureaucratic, and disconnected from the realities facing families and healthcare workers alike.
_**Because Connecticut does not just have a political problem.**_
_**It has a healthcare crisis.**_
And from nurse burnout to staffing shortages, collapsing trust in institutions, rising healthcare costs, and growing concerns over medical freedom, these problems are no longer isolated state issues.
**They require federal solutions.**
**Connecticut 's Nurses Are Burning Out**
Nurses are leaving the profession at alarming rates nationwide, and Connecticut is not immune.
The COVID era exposed what many healthcare workers already knew:
Healthcare systems often operate at maximum capacity with minimal margins for error.
Long shifts.
Understaffing.
Administrative overload.
Emotional exhaustion.
Violence against healthcare workers.
And increasing pressure from both government mandates and insurance-driven treatment decisions.
The people holding the system together are burning out.
And when nurses burn out, patient care suffers.
Families suffer.
Communities suffer.
No amount of political talking points can change that reality.
**When Bureaucrats Replace Doctors and Parents**
As a physician, I believe strongly in science, medicine, and public health.
But I also believe something else:
Medicine works best when patients trust their doctors -- not when families feel coerced by bureaucracies.
That trust has been damaged.
When government officials begin deciding what medical decisions are acceptable...
When insurance companies decide what treatments are "medically necessary" based on spreadsheets...
When parents feel their concerns are dismissed rather than heard...
We have a real problem.
And Connecticut families increasingly feel trapped between two systems:
Government bureaucracy and corporate healthcare bureaucracy.
Neither always acts in the best interest of the patient sitting in front of them.
That is why the debate over vaccine mandates, homeschooling oversight, and religious liberty resonates so deeply with many families.
It is not simply about one policy.
It is about whether ordinary people still maintain meaningful control over deeply personal decisions involving their children, health, and faith.
**Connecticut 's Homeschooling and Religious Liberty Debate**
Connecticut has become one of the central battlegrounds in the national conversation over parental rights and medical freedom.
The repeal of the religious exemption for school vaccines fundamentally changed the relationship between the state and families who objected on religious grounds.
Now many parents fear that government increasingly views disagreement itself as suspect.
At the same time, new conversations surrounding homeschooling oversight have created concern among families who believe parents -- not bureaucracies -- should remain primarily responsible for directing the education and upbringing of their children.
Recent legislative efforts and legal debates surrounding Connecticut's Religious Freedom Restoration Act have only intensified those concerns.
Many families are asking a larger question:
If constitutional liberties can be narrowed whenever politically inconvenient, what protections remain secure long term?
These are not fringe questions.
They are questions about trust.
And trust, once broken, is difficult to restore.
**Medical Freedom Does Not Mean Anti-Medicine**
One of the laziest attacks in modern politics is pretending every discussion about medical freedom is somehow anti-science.
It is not.
Patients deserve informed consent.
Parents deserve transparency.
Doctors deserve the ability to practice medicine without excessive political interference.
And healthcare workers deserve the right to raise legitimate concerns without fear of professional destruction.
A free society can support vaccines, medical innovation, and public health while still respecting constitutional liberties and parental rights.
Those principles are not mutually exclusive.
**Connecticut 's Problems Are Bigger Than Hartford**
Hartford alone cannot solve these problems.
Because many of the pressures driving healthcare dysfunction are federal in scope:
* Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement structures
* Insurance regulations
* Healthcare workforce shortages
* Pharmaceutical pricing
* Hospital consolidation
* Medical education costs
* Federal emergency preparedness policy
* Interstate licensing barriers
* Mental health infrastructure
* Supply chain vulnerabilities

These are national challenges requiring serious federal leadership.
That means electing people to Congress who actually understand healthcare from the inside -- not just through committee briefings and talking points.
Healthcare represents nearly 20% of America's GDP, yet very few members of Congress have direct frontline medical experience.
That disconnect matters.
Because policy written by career politicians often looks very different from the realities experienced by doctors, nurses, patients, and families.
**Mothers Understand Systems Failure Before Politicians Do**
Mothers are often the first to recognize when systems stop working.
They see it when appointments take months.
When pediatric specialists disappear.
When emergency rooms overflow.
When insurance denials delay treatment.
When burned-out nurses leave bedside care.
When schools, healthcare, and government institutions become less responsive and more ideological.
And they know instinctively that something fundamental is breaking down.
The answer is not simply more bureaucracy.
The answer is restoring trust, accountability, transparency, and human judgment to healthcare.
**A Mother 's Day Call to Action**
On this Mother's Day, we are asking mothers across Connecticut to become a **Mom for Michael**.
Join this grassroots movement and help take the fight for families, medical freedom, parental rights, and patient-centered healthcare from Hartford to the House.
We see your strength.
We see your advocacy.
We see what so many mothers across Connecticut have already done to stand up for their children, their families, and their values.
But now we have to carry that fight beyond Hartford.
We have to take it to Washington, D.C.
Because Connecticut's families need a doctor in the House -- someone who understands healthcare, respects mothers, trusts parents, and knows that medical decisions should belong to patients, families, and doctors, not bureaucrats or insurance companies.
This campaign is not powered by political machines.
It is powered by families.
By nurses.
By parents.
By exhausted healthcare workers.
By people who believe common sense still matters.
And by mothers who refuse to stay silent while the systems around them continue to fail.
**This Mother 's Day**
This Mother's Day should remind us of something simple:
Strong families require functional healthcare systems.
Functional healthcare systems require trust.
And trust cannot exist when families feel powerless inside systems controlled by bureaucrats, insurers, and political agendas.
Connecticut deserves better.
America deserves better.
And solving these challenges will require leadership willing to confront uncomfortable realities instead of managing headlines.
Because when bureaucrats decide medical decisions the same way insurance companies decide what is supposedly in your "best interests," we no longer have patient-centered healthcare.
We have institutionalized control masquerading as compassion.
And the people paying the price are families, healthcare workers, and the next generation.
This Mother's Day, be a **Mom for Michael**.
Help us bring this grassroots movement to Congress.
Support the movement today at

clean_body: Taking the Fight to Washington DC | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more The Goldstein Substack - A Father-Son Publication Dr. Goldstein is Running for Congress and his page can be found Taking the Fight to Washington DC | | | May 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Mother's Day is supposed to be about gratitude. It is supposed to be about honoring the women who raise families, hold communities together, care for aging parents, and increasingly serve on the front lines of America's healthcare system. But this year, many mothers in Connecticut are exhausted. Not simply from parenting. From navigating a system that feels increasingly impersonal, bureaucratic, and disconnected from the realities facing families and healthcare workers alike. Because Connecticut does not just have a political problem. It has a healthcare crisis. And from nurse burnout to staffing shortages, collapsing trust in institutions, rising healthcare costs, and growing concerns over medical freedom, these problems are no longer isolated state issues. They require federal solutions. Connecticut 's Nurses Are Burning Out Nurses are leaving the profession at alarming rates nationwide, and Connecticut is not immune. The COVID era exposed what many healthcare workers already knew: Healthcare systems often operate at maximum capacity with minimal margins for error. Long shifts. Understaffing. Administrative overload. Emotional exhaustion. Violence against healthcare workers. And increasing pressure from both government mandates and insurance-driven treatment decisions. The people holding the system together are burning out. And when nurses burn out, patient care suffers. Families suffer. Communities suffer. No amount of political talking points can change that reality. When Bureaucrats Replace Doctors and Parents As a physician, I believe strongly in science, medicine, and public health. But I also believe something else: Medicine works best when patients trust their doctors -- not when families feel coerced by bureaucracies. That trust has been damaged. When government officials begin deciding what medical decisions are acceptable... When insurance companies decide what treatments are "medically necessary" based on spreadsheets... When parents feel their concerns are dismissed rather than heard... We have a real problem. And Connecticut families increasingly feel trapped between two systems: Government bureaucracy and corporate healthcare bureaucracy. Neither always acts in the best interest of the patient sitting in front of them. That is why the debate over vaccine mandates, homeschooling oversight, and religious liberty resonates so deeply with many families. It is not simply about one policy. It is about whether ordinary people still maintain meaningful control over deeply personal decisions involving their children, health, and faith. Connecticut 's Homeschooling and Religious Liberty Debate Connecticut has become one of the central battlegrounds in the national conversation over parental rights and medical freedom. The repeal of the religious exemption for school vaccines fundamentally changed the relationship between the state and families who objected on religious grounds. Now many parents fear that government increasingly views disagreement itself as suspect. At the same time, new conversations surrounding homeschooling oversight have created concern among families who believe parents -- not bureaucracies -- should remain primarily responsible for directing the education and upbringing of their children. Recent legislative efforts and legal debates surrounding Connecticut's Religious Freedom Restoration Act have only intensified those concerns. Many families are asking a larger question: If constitutional liberties can be narrowed whenever politically inconvenient, what protections remain secure long term? These are not fringe questions. They are questions about trust. And trust, once broken, is difficult to restore. Medical Freedom Does Not Mean Anti-Medicine One of the laziest attacks in modern politics is pretending every discussion about medical freedom is somehow anti-science. It is not. Patients deserve informed consent. Parents deserve transparency. Doctors deserve the ability to practice medicine without excessive political interference. And healthcare workers deserve the right to raise legitimate concerns without fear of professional destruction. A free society can support vaccines, medical innovation, and public health while still respecting constitutional liberties and parental rights. Those principles are not mutually exclusive. Connecticut 's Problems Are Bigger Than Hartford Hartford alone cannot solve these problems. Because many of the pressures driving healthcare dysfunction are federal in scope: Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement structures Insurance regulations Healthcare workforce shortages Pharmaceutical pricing Hospital consolidation Medical education costs Federal emergency preparedness policy Interstate licensing barriers Mental health infrastructure Supply chain vulnerabilities These are national challenges requiring serious federal leadership. That means electing people to Congress who actually understand healthcare from the inside -- not just through committee briefings and talking points. Healthcare represents nearly 20% of America's GDP, yet very few members of Congress have direct frontline medical experience. That disconnect matters. Because policy written by career politicians often looks very different from the realities experienced by doctors, nurses, patients, and families. Mothers Understand Systems Failure Before Politicians Do Mothers are often the first to recognize when systems stop working. They see it when appointments take months. When pediatric specialists disappear. When emergency rooms overflow. When insurance denials delay treatment. When burned-out nurses leave bedside care. When schools, healthcare, and government institutions become less responsive and more ideological. And they know instinctively that something fundamental is breaking down. The answer is not simply more bureaucracy. The answer is restoring trust, accountability, transparency, and human judgment to healthcare. A Mother 's Day Call to Action On this Mother's Day, we are asking mothers across Connecticut to become a Mom for Michael. Join this grassroots movement and help take the fight for families, medical freedom, parental rights, and patient-centered healthcare from Hartford to the House. We see your strength. We see your advocacy. We see what so many mothers across Connecticut have already done to stand up for their children, their families, and their values. But now we have to carry that fight beyond Hartford. We have to take it to Washington, D.C. Because Connecticut's families need a doctor in the House -- someone who understands healthcare, respects mothers, trusts parents, and knows that medical decisions should belong to patients, families, and doctors, not bureaucrats or insurance companies. This campaign is not powered by political machines. It is powered by families. By nurses. By parents. By exhausted healthcare workers. By people who believe common sense still matters. And by mothers who refuse to stay silent while the systems around them continue to fail. This Mother 's Day This Mother's Day should remind us of something simple: Strong families require functional healthcare systems. Functional healthcare systems require trust. And trust cannot exist when families feel powerless inside systems controlled by bureaucrats, insurers, and political agendas. Connecticut deserves better. America deserves better. And solving these challenges will require leadership willing to confront uncomfortable realities instead of managing headlines. Because when bureaucrats decide medical decisions the same way insurance companies decide what is supposedly in your "best interests," we no longer have patient-centered healthcare. We have institutionalized control masquerading as compassion. And the people paying the price are families, healthcare workers, and the next generation. This Mother's Day, be a Mom for Michael**. Help us bring this grassroots movement to Congress. Support the movement today at Please share this as this movement must grow out of the circle and into the entire state. The Goldstein Substack is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell The Goldstein Substack that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Michael Goldstein ? ?

name: Nellie Pou
subject: Register to vote before the primary election
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pout-header-3.png]

Friend,

In case you didn't know, the voter registration deadline for the primary election is May 12th — just 2 days away!

If you haven’t already, make sure to register to vote before the deadline and talk with your family, friends, and neighbors about registering to vote. Many races can come down to just a few votes, so every vote counts!

This year's elections are critical, so I encourage you to visit New Jersey's Voter Information Portal [https://www.nj.gov/state/elections/voter-registration.shtml] to check your status, register, and find out more information about where and how to vote in next month's primary election.

VISIT THE PORTAL [https://www.nj.gov/state/elections/voter-registration.shtml]

Thank you for using your voice,

Nellie Pou
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP61/1/115406/images/pou_headshot_crop.jpg]

About Nellie Pou
Nellie Pou is the first Latina to represent New Jersey in Congress, and will be a target for the NRCC in 2026. As a mother and grandmother, Nellie knows what New Jersey families are facing and will continue to bring down costs while creating good-paying jobs. Nellie has dedicated her entire life to the community that raised her, and in Congress she will do the same.
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?refcode=emailfooter&refcode2=e5cfc5c2-934c-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640] Give Monthly [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pou_em?recurring=true&refcode=footermonthly&refcode2=e5cfc5c2-934c-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640]
Follow Nellie
https://x.com/NelliePou [https://x.com/NelliePou] https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/ [https://www.facebook.com/senatorpou/] https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/ [https://www.instagram.com/congresswomannelliepou/]
Checks may also be mailed to:
Nellie Pou for Congress - PO BOX 2696, Paterson, NJ, 07509 Paid for by Nellie Pou for Congress Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/QcG518LlG0yfilVoJO5ZLg2?unsubscribedata=62nLjlTWmIjJyfVqkKGo8iFUZzljJWmdD0Dqqt3UJ7nVvaK0Mjh4LVEaeACAqKb8rDAF9irZRhC1AUQQe%2F630Vz9gT1RN6QEpjrGKwzP6ROO7zMrNQl8ydqwwK%2F0sqGpFSwiMH%2B7FYseYhO83jYSldzF1sZ7YVNh09ahaS4%2B%2Bq6WJvuQ9fstCSzCTYfcZE3dfiLinkQLPB0TQPbZgPaQFEGreFN31Xd%2Bwxo517CYZgcvx9Y13%2B7cfXtyY61RcftbixNx89Q4cHq1THpLtE5j64HBmf6OwJxxzlCauA3LDuJwM7J4SgsC7eiogUKBS3GDpyDR1JfytoxPLeg3UNmZd32y1EoMk621YfeywNDHbdwIm2tmgGHw1lAD4RyxugFye4bZ5Opuiu1O%2FSD6iRZLdTTMdkHEW3wvdK55mw%2FaQbmArGrjJshxrONn%2BUilJokf] . name: Mike Thompson
subject: Peter, Happy Mother’s Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Today we celebrate Mother’s Day. We wouldn’t be the people we are today without all of our hardworking mothers. Whether that’s your mom, your grandmother, your spouse, your daughter, or any other mother in your life, we recognize the impact they have.

Mothers sacrifice so much to give us a better life. Mothers deserve more than a single day of recognition. They deserve policies that actually make their lives easier, and that's what I'm working on every day I'm in office. But today is their day.

Let's celebrate the mothers who shape our lives in ways big and small. From my family to yours, wishing you a peaceful and love-filled Mother’s Day.

Thanks,

Mike Thompson
Authorized & Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support.mikethompsonforcongress.com/p/KK6Ox2NzGk6ccCSbCGuKYA2?unsubscribedata=8ftWm%2Bm287Enq9HTIourlameJu5kQvBFDQzTVZwAFLv%2BAtEh8Kbir0stpDEfCSfLh8Yn0qDr0Vxi9M9oYxbm20u1hCkI9WjS%2Brl3sZ7b1hcNSZ3Rl9gwtY%2FW%2BwyJocLoKyyJ8UAe2XFWPrrvFdU1iBGiEyIAWUO3orJ9DUhUmoOibG7te5zt4uyaOKlA1pYlwwBz6GyF3vG3pnGKYOvvlMWeOkwDqEtoREmgKF7E0CAamkISND%2Bwv8djDJ6%2FQiZElbaFombDoNaksiiFYnYVt7RkLzBYH4tg3EBuFabE1oMk6YaeV4cTUMJtvrZ3DDrR3qVJQT9mzymiXc1xEaDTLaJlL6eWQn4m9xNvsNypX3SyicHC5WkbPjkKo2EV6jTb7Kpjo2Mk7wYP9lJyTAEux3lcyB6qXqPjuhNrjUD16kE%3D Mike Thompson for Congress

name: JamesTalarico.com
subject: A teacher, HEB worker, librarian…and you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
When James launched his first campaign, he made a rare decision in Texas politics. He rejected corporate PAC money and was the only member of the state legislature willing to do it.
So when it came time to think about how we’d raise money for a U.S. Senate race — one expected to be among the most expensive in the entire country, we were confronted with the same decision.
But it was an easy one. James will never let Texans doubt who he represents. His vote will never be for sale — even when it hurts our fundraising. You simply can’t run a grassroots campaign for the people while selling Texans out to corporate interests.
So James put his faith in this grassroots movement and the people of Texas.
And from day one, the response was overwhelming. Within minutes of launching, an HEB worker named Ashley from Fort Worth gave $25. Then Marisol, a teacher from El Paso, made a $5 contribution, and moments later, Jeremy, a librarian from McAllen, gave a $10 recurring donation...and we were off.
It’s these folks and people like you who are going to ensure we have the resources to take on our Republican opponent and his billionaire and corporate mega-donors. When everyone who believes in this movement pitches in, it all adds up.
**[So please, if you can, make a $5 donation to our campaign today to take back Texas. Only a small percentage of James Talarico supporters will choose to give today, so your support is that much more important.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q2.t_pReT&refcode=em260510-1-ak&t=1&akid=628%2E1347274%2E9upswO)**

name: Chaz Molder
subject: Happy Mother’s Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Molder for Congress
body:
💐

| | | | |
---
Hi Peter, Before the day gets away from me, I just wanted to say happy Mother’s Day to all the moms out there. I’ve been lucky to be surrounded by incredible mothers my whole life. My own mom helped shape who I am through her love, patience, and steady support. This year, Mother’s Day is a little more difficult for me personally. It’s the first Mother’s Day without my mom. I know she’d be proud. [](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/VsJ9_Isp0W2HTbVAtpRgTg~~/AAUS1hA~/mvuOShVOQDtZ_u7lSl8EUzg0fDfTVQlcyQ0si4ToEPk8oMAYXAPsBHQ7MMXyDKl_jiq4W_4eKA93WDMlZOgJ0qfyvyIVY05FYcYZVAs5KA4mK8Z-WCbBWJaqO-TI1lajQv-UFVrvx4D_3yRscFSARN4AKXkf069t6YoDs4UBlD4A9Q1MrZD6fnjeJQ2MVD_dJyGXwg0PjTMiKdWOIfp2i08OYN0Q1LoNk8KpKlwBrCjhaWOd3rSyPGkQL1eDNmpxLnXlQGNqc8DSLkOGRB4DhQ~~) Mother’s Day will always be a special celebration at our house because our kids have such a great mom. Of all of the decisions I’ve made, marrying Liz has been the very best. [](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/DHBJ3uUHLBawZizDmqJHnQ~~/AAUS1hA~/mvuOShVOQDtZ_u7lSl8EUzg0fDfTVQlcyQ0si4ToEPk8oMAYXAPsBHQ7MMXyDKl_jiq4W_4eKA93WDMlZOgJ0qfyvyIVY05FYcYZVAs5KA4mK8Z-WCbBWJaqO-TI1lajQv-UFVrvx4D_3yRscFSARN4AKXkf069t6YoDs4UBlD4A9Q1MrZD6fnjeJQ2MVD_dlEB9OnFsFt0H0UMIaHPMDKNTWe5zjMvYSGQJ7nQQCuIlBDBYewDFRYMo2TVhICahY0eYgaHtailyY6UdggujMA~~) [](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/fQ6Ww2d7lTrhAuPEyECPZA~~/AAUS1hA~/mvuOShVOQDtZ_u7lSl8EUzg0fDfTVQlcyQ0si4ToEPk8oMAYXAPsBHQ7MMXyDKl_jiq4W_4eKA93WDMlZOgJ0qfyvyIVY05FYcYZVAs5KA4mK8Z-WCbBWJaqO-TI1lajQv-UFVrvx4D_3yRscFSARN4AKXkf069t6YoDs4UBlD4A9Q1MrZD6fnjeJQ2MVD_dI6KdtHd-KZCrAEy6LgeqW0sU8FmfBJuQyOYe46zgihj9eMwKsWsoYOAXKL27AbS65qV0UIz0BlvFpNhOlgYElw~~) Mothers do so much that often goes unseen. The early mornings, the late nights, the rides to school and practices, the encouragement after hard days, the constant love and support that keep families going. Today’s a good reminder to slow down for a moment and say thank you. So whether you’re spending today with family, remembering someone you love, or just trying to make it through a busy Sunday, I hope you know how appreciated you are. Happy Mother’s Day. — Chaz | | |
---|---|---
### About Chaz Molder
Chaz Molder is the Mayor of Columbia, Tennessee, serving since 2019 and re-elected in 2022. A Columbia native and graduate of the University of Tennessee and the University of Memphis Law School, he returned home to build a career in law and public service. As mayor, he founded the Columbia Mayor’s Youth Council to promote civic engagement among young people and emphasizes the importance of being present and responsive to the community. | | [ Donate Now ](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/lSs8D0x1KQYgFC2bW5V22w~~/AAUS1hA~/ASD_qxX5YOFym_4T90ubVQj18zQ3z-eyYCe38A4vvXxoegrImY41AwwIxorxYGs9IRSLQlW_y8ASDdCVpWd3GkhH0-EMKBzoaR3R0PP0MmkcnGGi7Flsdw8KENL1v3hlhAJ8zyrTThc0zuyXjCIHikefDye7UiwYKxY95e1B8momGQ-iTWu3ull32xXiISWoi4d7Mwe2AmtyawGY9OyvPWC62yH3Ozg6kzNQWb8C9VYegYI3bb_FC4DbgfHVsMdw)

clean_body: 💐 | | | | | Hi Peter, Before the day gets away from me, I just wanted to say happy Mother’s Day to all the moms out there. I’ve been lucky to be surrounded by incredible mothers my whole life. My own mom helped shape who I am through her love, patience, and steady support. This year, Mother’s Day is a little more difficult for me personally. It’s the first Mother’s Day without my mom. I know she’d be proud. ~~ Mother’s Day will always be a special celebration at our house because our kids have such a great mom. Of all of the decisions I’ve made, marrying Liz has been the very best. ~~ ~~ Mothers do so much that often goes unseen. The early mornings, the late nights, the rides to school and practices, the encouragement after hard days, the constant love and support that keep families going. Today’s a good reminder to slow down for a moment and say thank you. So whether you’re spending today with family, remembering someone you love, or just trying to make it through a busy Sunday, I hope you know how appreciated you are. Happy Mother’s Day. — Chaz | | | ---|---|--- About Chaz Molder Chaz Molder is the Mayor of Columbia, Tennessee, serving since 2019 and re-elected in 2022. A Columbia native and graduate of the University of Tennessee and the University of Memphis Law School, he returned home to build a career in law and public service. As mayor, he founded the Columbia Mayor’s Youth Council to promote civic engagement among young people and emphasizes the importance of being present and responsive to the community. | | | ~~ Click~~ to receive fewer emails. Follow Chaz | ~ | | ---|---|--- | Paid for by Molder for Congress Molder for CongressPO Box 1468Columbia, TN 38402United States www.chazmolder.com "Website"

**The goal is simple. Bankrupt the opposition. Silence anyone who fights back.**

**We need to be ready to fight back, Friend.**

Lawfare is real. It is organized. And it has already destroyed careers, silenced dissent, and punished patriots for nothing more than answering the call to serve.

We watched it happen to J6 defendants. We watched it happen to ICE agents. Now, it’s about to happen to everyone working within the Trump administration.

The weapon is the same. Only the target shifts.

The Kash Foundation exists to fight it — wherever it appears, whoever it's aimed at.

**That is exactly why we created Mission First Legal.** Mission First Legal represents service members, veterans, and federal officers who acted lawfully, honorably, and in good faith, yet found themselves targeted for standing in the way of a political narrative.

These patriots answered the call. _The Kash Foundation will not abandon them_.

Pritzker and his allies have unlimited resources and a coordinated machine behind them. [We have you, Friend. And right now, we need your help more than ever.](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkL1urDAUhJ_GdCD_gaFwcaUV3d42JTrYh8VZYyNzIMrbR7tSHiD1jGY-fR_36Q4hhvT4DxvaQstUjmmFC7_zuSIUD3M-aS_5Ex3JSXLZ1bytBX_1ROWtdx6Eq9AKY3qttFJDtVqOUrXQ9a6flYbW99gNvteza1ujndJVsK8p3goujJLCNGLQYmg5Qm-UUXxhmkPEQk3B_ZxjcJAIjueSi8PG5a2KdiXaD6b-MTkyOdKK9ROOtV7ymTxQyKkpeF2Ny0yOO9Q-JyBkajxpmxxsO4RHYur2C8Jk905yIkzE1K3QIqti_f4VYgxHZ5jmjw1CfP_T321dVv4EAAD__3KkeAI)

They are coming, and they are counting on good people like you to do nothing.

I refuse to let that happen. I hope you will, too, Friend.

For America’s patriots,

Andrew Ollis

subject: Simple math

body:
The GOP has a 53-47 majority in the Senate. THIS is how we will restore Democratic control...
#
The math is simple, Peter. Let’s talk through it:
**Republicans currently hold a 53-47 majority in the U.S. Senate.**
**Democrats need a net gain of four seats to take control.**
That might sound like a lot. But look at the map right now:
We’re already favored to flip **North Carolina** – an open seat currently held by the GOP. **Maine** and **Ohio** are both GOP-held seats rated as TOSS-UPS.
That’s three seats we feel pretty good about.
And we haven’t even mentioned **Alaska** , **Iowa** , or **Texas** – states that are suddenly, shockingly in play for 2026.
**[FLIP THE SENATE: CHIP IN NOW](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103907?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0)**
**_Cook Political Report_ recently shifted FOUR Senate ratings in Democrats' direction, calling the likeliest outcome a one-to-three seat Democratic pickup – and noting that four seats, a full majority, is within reach.**
The environment is ripe. We’ve got strong candidates running in each of these races. Now, the only thing we need is the resources to compete in every single one of these races and MORE.
November will be here before we know it. That’s why we need your help:
**[So PLEASE, Peter, will you chip in $15 or more today to fund our fight to flip all of these seats and more across the country this year?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103907?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $5](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=5&t=3&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Contribute $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=15&t=4&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=5&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=6&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=7&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103908?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=8&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/103907?refcode=em20260510-10415&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=9&akid=10415%2E614464%2EujHee0)

subject: What we promised
body:
---
Derek, **FIRST:** We promised our Senate candidates in key battleground states, state parties, and our organizing teams on the ground that we’d do everything we could to make sure that they all have the resources they need to win this November. **THEN:** Fundraising slowed down. _A lot._ We wish we had better news, but fundraising has been slow across the board this month. **NOW:****We’re on track to fall short of our $300,000 mid-month fundraising goal, and we’re asking you to**[**chip in $7 today to help us keep our promise.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?amount=7&refcode=em_20260510_7066_fr&refcode2=7066_22930333_kMVinC&akid=7066%2E22930333%2EkMVinC)** **[CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?amount=7&refcode=em_20260510_7066_fr&refcode2=7066_22930333_kMVinC&akid=7066%2E22930333%2EkMVinC)** If you’ve been looking for an easy way to make a real impact on the midterm elections, this is it. The DNC is the _only_ organization focused on electing Democrats up and down the ballot, across the country, _every year._ As we speak, the DNC is hiring organizers nationwide who will register tens of thousands of new Democrats in battleground states. We’re investing in state parties, we’re fighting to protect voting rights in court, and **we’re building the largest midterm operation in DNC history.** [**But we can’t do it alone. If you’re with us, please chip in $7 to the DNC today. We’ll put your donation to work immediately to elect Democrats up and down the ballot.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?amount=7&refcode=em_20260510_7066_fr&refcode2=7066_22930333_kMVinC&akid=7066%2E22930333%2EkMVinC) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-may-2026-gfr?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260510_7066_fr&refcode2=7066_22930333_kMVinC&akid=7066%2E22930333%2EkMVinC)**

name: Kimberly Bretches
subject: My husband saved Austin. Now he’s facing 99 years in prison.
body:
|
---
[ ](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=95189ef82b9f4448892f220deed3804f&_e=AMIrkkOjjGGyNGAnoukcwGKXPHSwDsRLMH5d7qlq19fYCK2YTyvElWakzu8HKQfmYykeEvZplHer1syfP6YMS7yvm-XLpLguEeXYfF4ob3DhIeXx-92Ld5eKm1XvLNCdG_UiSGPxXc1GlmWeqdsA2H7R47Zl6DiLHfpMqYDzQ0WoMKQjOsooSvB1c9W1FM3moIg85cISvD6r9KbjTZRTGKyuPbwowkmfteBXQpZLZ2Z4dXACDlT1DCZ_PEYV8P602ZXMzThnLzZEgTIJeQEgtcthmJAXVrw6vnCFK7dauTZ8E23p8kyBCua7E-gBYTzfHK22K5qfkMH_6crJliKWDh70F0_7cvPRyM8CuEQ49kMV86RqA2GA4N17jFYY6b0M5D8HWCYkaaNBGiUlOryjzkBKEUg_XVLRN0JgZBTlEIdzrViExAzgNxWLaOZDzxqj) Officer Chance Bretches and his family — Austin, Texas
* * *
Dear Friend, My name is Kimberly Bretches. I'm a wife and a mother of four young children. And I'm writing to you today because I am out of options and I am terrified. I'll be honest, asking strangers for help doesn't come naturally to me. But neither does the idea of my husband spending the rest of his life in prison for doing his job — so here I am. Chance is a police officer in Austin, Texas. He is, without question, the best man I know. He has spent his career keeping dangerous people off the streets, protecting a city that doesn't always make it easy to do so, and coming home every night to a family that needs him. His supervisors have praised him for his professionalism, for his instincts, for his dedication. He is exactly the kind of officer you want showing up when things go wrong. | By every measure, Chance did everything right. He used department-approved tools, following his training, to protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin.
---
And in May of 2020, things went very wrong in Austin. For three consecutive days, our city was under siege. Violent agitators hurled rocks, frozen water bottles, and exploding fireworks directly at police officers. Buildings were vandalized and looted. Police vehicles were set on fire. A rioter was caught with a Molotov cocktail. Officers were injured. The city was teetering on the edge of complete chaos, and everyone knew it.
On May 31st, the third day, when the mob was larger and more dangerous than it had ever been, Chance was on the front lines. He did what he was trained to do. He deployed non-lethal, department-approved beanbag projectiles to stop the aggression and protect his fellow officers and the people of Austin. The crowd eventually dispersed. The city held.
**No one questioned what he did that night. His supervisors praised his response. His department stood behind him.**
And then Austin elected District Attorney José Garza.
Instead of holding the rioters accountable — the ones who threw the fireworks, who lit the fires, who carried the Molotov cocktails — Garza turned his attention to the officers who stopped them. He launched an indiscriminate campaign against law enforcement, using the power of his office not to protect the public, but to make political examples out of good cops.
Chance became one of his targets.
| Chance now faces up to 99 Years in prison — for following his training.
---
My husband now faces up to 99 years in prison. Not because he did anything wrong. Not because his department found fault with his actions. Not because any credible review of that night concluded he acted improperly. But because one politically-motivated prosecutor decided the officers who held the line deserved to be punished for holding it. This case has been grinding through the system for years now. The trial has already been delayed several times and is **now scheduled for June of this year.** Every continuance, every filing, every hearing means more hours of legal work, and more bills our family has to find a way to pay. I am asking for your help because we cannot do this alone. The National Center for Police Defense has been in Chance's corner, and your donation goes toward funding his legal defense, ensuring he has the representation he deserves as he walks into that courtroom in June.
| [DONATE NOW TO SUPPORT CHANCE ›](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=95189ef82b9f4448892f220deed3804f&_e=AMIrkkOjjGGyNGAnoukcwGKXPHSwDsRLMH5d7qlq19fYCK2YTyvElWakzu8HKQfmYykeEvZplHer1syfP6YMS7yvm-XLpLguEeXYfF4ob3Bo9bQsBF0kJPr1Lqh3mtMrWSpaNY1BQvaQjBapufWmK-pSu6rhwHqS25sHAOiGflUQOkvGFlX52_w8-CDam4AZOACSyvlDEmp_r3_Mxxr5WhSSfVetq5kKlJmR2cCw0BXZgHZ7YYj0FdSVt7s_XuI_Owbp33W1cuRiV571JWVDmtS8YWTWDheaCihvQUd2tFwy_DtaYYTiKnIudviiRPxK-nD_hx1cdPvHJ2CdOa6F-GuAbA2tEeHbEYQPwn3iJ478uw4XYqzRGr8GobZnaMW_XjUfO4ClSeYXpMCbcDIQKZVhwRR-V0tpJv0eEvJlezELh9t6OfCodQtDSQ_pHQb2)

If you believe that the men and women who run toward danger deserve better than this, [please give today](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=95189ef82b9f4448892f220deed3804f&_e=AMIrkkOjjGGyNGAnoukcwGKXPHSwDsRLMH5d7qlq19fYCK2YTyvElWakzu8HKQfmYykeEvZplHer1syfP6YMS7yvm-XLpLguEeXYfF4ob3CuyxshbB9kBZ6lYHFQz9Q7Yryu9pnP3Cqs2F1qdi5HirYuuwUQpa_eGo92O8nAC-64PsSRIx_QsXmg-qwvFj0CyY2BBHmKcA2gXbdcxzETlXvJTsNRDlrBifhaA6cxG7JFa1Oix0gENPXfTy6E3C9Z4EsSoYD2jOgU08-j0sMO0sg-GKcC4sFgQKa2q0NcaDjL5ZX5pAJAmUQL0PBrD4gNl_Llm2bfDSVqVUV7Irgq3t-njzAVG0Fk8EnckQSTYlNpMja5vRqwJ2jb2RCcm6IVyyDC4HP12gY9QKiedFzF91W5r8-ehLpBaDm8YbS0eoGLvpAo_wySymD91IYkCMRf). Whatever you can. No amount is too small, and our family will not forget it. Chance stood between Austin and chaos on May 31st, 2020. He didn't hesitate. He didn't run. He did his job. Now I'm asking you to stand with him. With gratitude and hope, **Kimberly Bretches** Wife of Officer Chance Bretches, Austin Police Department
| **P.S.** Chance's trial is this June. We are closer than we've ever been to getting Chance in front of a jury that will hear the truth, but we need the resources to get there. **[Please donate today and help us finish this fight.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=95189ef82b9f4448892f220deed3804f&_e=AMIrkkOjjGGyNGAnoukcwGKXPHSwDsRLMH5d7qlq19fYCK2YTyvElWakzu8HKQfmYykeEvZplHer1syfP6YMS7yvm-XLpLguEeXYfF4ob3CkOKF5aC0UWO4Oe6oYwIT0VZi2JCnsQb4RZMAfLzDBSQ3N1kRotyE3OtVVOOyeJz2wy02cAfL5fx1JkKJym63yMRyupLleiygi0R73nkWm3tY4oWf2vaqwL9SYs91gt6Wz1aIyhrASbkLHWZNBYtXXIEV07cYC9dmnb8TGFh241xhniNzjdxfKMosiP1j8PBFf7E6yai59G1Et_0pbh6xflhzFDT4VPl9iNBPVhKKf9Bk1ELorkIRLvSDeaAKyYNiE_tYPmxr8cCd3FiyhS6t5ZjXpf3NfJmGiK0ppFoZzgYO9bG7xUShxlnYAQFeAjAIveznSbEfjetuG3lP1lldf) **

name: Doris Matsui
subject: Supporting moms today, and every day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Matsui for Congress
body:
Being a mother has been one of the most meaningful experiences of my life–and one of my greatest responsibilities. | | | [](http://links.matsuiforcongress.com/s/c/7Zf2h7xPg9yx0V-1zPZdtuvXlPya5OeLps9JPcZeUc5lXQiD1QhbE5DhVOmK_CkLNFdvvXJDHr_25PIhIxKz-EYAg6FvSoC1PpoHtKAtnygbPcdNLqAkJVe-pGIhvqIO4s704KY_ZVKgnIuceml6MafJSXa0ry9XkhYM4musYTN0AN9MlZCdhlHPpdYe90jKbglPnclldbXu6DxUc1hOxHXPCxfVoqYaaIfCHwr_rPvHkcLsB3lNJOBXvganyD_8bLNtcI9PHrTM3O6hm_WhEshIqACaSrgh7aMIEhN-E0giQqexOVQgmuMXCYBUXQdyYZQJEsNEx3xUCGRioqWRmP2C80ic_LeMF1iJ9_DZjH2XMz5b9oAnhSII5NWSzJeG81V62CmtrmvjXPBg4SI82CzP_nAWOPhxQ4RWkh-nmEpnCRcf-MgatJVi0-a9vHsYQXQxYX1ya7ETs2_7h_JbYLo0_Y6rFgDqpJkhoPgEemDVoX3cuD3yz4-CSRufQJ7Yv3lahsD0R1v9ypZcLDy7K-VXmYoRC-S1_Ob7Zugb6AnJ1q8iJ4Uv9lg5btCeAmlYxzyEIq9jccHssGQXa3D6MjL9MBuQfNOhwtkHvFUwnVVhf7vVDTREt2AX358ZMQH6ppk5oZbhuS-Jky8mhwW2Bvs2KxxmnhRdjAz4bnS0iNhWHTiUOxudwBDvGpbhJQycQOHp-RAO9jtHCORy6YZ34GyJe9zer5hDCa89fRnRAHZdIACIgAOOHEofAww5n2TaAverOqBej61AANIQQeBTb-JaUuTCOE-83w0Pybv73PVf9L3zXgNmlFrutVX3xHk4XT2Kmnp4y07L4AFEcUhB89-6wNkkCxUdQmch1vLZQPGN3e6omB7oLw7hr_cy0Z0RwuWsbAGhsc8ESHcQ2g/OsGaWSw5UAoRUiRwJYEA3A23LAqwcYnw/20) Happy Mother’s Day, friend! Today, we honor every mother, grandmother, and mother figure. Being a mother has been one of the most meaningful experiences of my life–and one of my greatest responsibilities. Like so many mothers, I’ve spent every day doing what’s right for my family. And that same commitment has guided my work—to help build a better future for our children and grandchildren. Across the country, mothers are working tirelessly to support their families and give their children every opportunity to succeed. Mothers do it all—and then some. But many are struggling to keep up with rising costs. Every family deserves to be able to afford groceries, quality healthcare, and have the opportunity to thrive. That’s why I’m committed to lowering costs, expanding access to childcare, fighting for affordable healthcare, and creating opportunities to help working families not just get by, but get ahead. This is what every mother and family deserves. clean_body: Being a mother has been one of the most meaningful experiences of my life–and one of my greatest responsibilities. | | | Happy Mother’s Day, friend! Today, we honor every mother, grandmother, and mother figure. Being a mother has been one of the most meaningful experiences of my life–and one of my greatest responsibilities. Like so many mothers, I’ve spent every day doing what’s right for my family. And that same commitment has guided my work—to help build a better future for our children and grandchildren. Across the country, mothers are working tirelessly to support their families and give their children every opportunity to succeed. Mothers do it all—and then some. But many are struggling to keep up with rising costs. Every family deserves to be able to afford groceries, quality healthcare, and have the opportunity to thrive. That’s why I’m committed to lowering costs, expanding access to childcare, fighting for affordable healthcare, and creating opportunities to help working families not just get by, but get ahead. This is what every mother and family deserves. Best, Doris Matsui As a Member of Congress, Doris works to ensure that the voices of her district’s constituents are heard in Congress, and fights for the needs of the Sacramento region. Paid for by Matsui for Congress

name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: HAPPY MOTHER'S DAY -- A Tribute to My Mother in Heaven
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lilytangwilliams/happy-mothers-day-a-tribute-to-my-mother-in-heaven?e=c338950e03)
---
| |
---
| |
# HAPPY MOTHER'S DAY - A Tribute to My Mother in Heaven

My mother Huai Bin passed away in Colorado, November 2020 at the age of 77.

Her life in China was tough, and it took me a long time to really comprehend her life and death because I had to confront the complex feelings and meaning of her legacy she gave to me.

I loved my mother for her kindness and her gentle soul. She was meek, often in ways I used to not approve of or understand: passive and submissive, she was conflict averse. Her interactions with Chinese Communist Party officials were characterized by obedience and passive tolerance of their inhumane treatment of people like herself.

For decades I was haunted by a childhood memory of when she got on her knees and begged a CCP official for a raise at her factory job. She sacrificed her dignity only to be cruelly denied. So ashamed I was of her unwillingness to fight, to just take it. My father, on the other hand, was a fighter whose strength was like the factory steel he helped produce: firm, resilient, and tough. I spent my youth assuming that my defiant nature was only from my father. Now I know that I was mistaken.

In an unremarkable village in Penshan County, Sichuan Province, China, a sickly child cursed by fate was born prematurely. Her mother believed her too weak to survive and left her to die in peace, but she did not die. Fighting through labored breathing, she produced a cry so loud and enduring that her mother could no longer ignore the wailing. She was fed that night and would live to see the morning.

Growing up poor in Chengdu, Sichuan, barely able to read with an rudamentary elementary level education and an introverted personality, she resigned herself to a life of labor in a state-run factory. The level of her social condition was matched only by her stature: at 4'9", she was frail and severely nearsighted. Despite this, she won the love and adoration of my father, a tough street-fighting illiterate orphan whom she married and was later blessed with three children.

Raising children under Mao’s regime was an arduous burden. We lived in a primitive worker’s row house with eight other families sharing an outhouse. We were literally dirt-poor; our mud floor would sometimes sprout mushrooms after flooding from the nearby Jin river. We barely survived on food rationing coupons.

Despite all this, my mother’s heart never hardened. She was known for approaching beggars in the street with small gifts and the words: “Buddha bless you!” Her generosity defied the cruelty of her reality.

Amid a very hard life, she would find happiness in music (singing after a little Chinese moonshine). She was elegant, clean, and loved pretty, pink clothing, which was frowned upon under Mao’s regime. Using what little money she saved to buy pink fabrics was a small act of defiance against fate.

Happiness was often short-lived, however, as she was ill most of the time and required frequent care in the decrepit state-run local hospitals, a product of socialized medicine. While undergoing surgery in the 1980s, she received a blood transfusion infected with syphilis, a discovery made a decade later after I first brought my mother to the United States in 1995.

Despite a course of antibiotics, her brain had already suffered the consequences of neurosyphilis. She displayed signs of dementia at the age of 59. Though she was ill, America offered my mother a degree of peace, even if it was momentary.

She and my father converted to Christianity and lived peacefully, attending a Chinese Christian church every Sunday. In the end, her advanced dementia collided with COVID-19, and after being hospitalized, she died alone in November 2020.

My mother’s legacy is one of defiance. It began with a cry that defied death, lived through compassion that defied pain, and endures in me. This revelation testifies to the miracle of God’s design — subtle, yet purposeful. God imbues in each of us a part of his divine essence.

For my mother, she bore the suffering and submission to pain that Christ knew; not out of weakness, but out of love for her family, so that they would survive. She embraced God’s Word that we defy evil not through the hardening of our hearts, but through turning the other cheek and committing to compassion.

Happy Mother's Day. I love you, Mom. I know you are very proud of me running for Congress in NH and fighting for all mothers and daughters. I pray for all the mothers in the world to never suffer or endure your tragic fate under communism.

---

subject: Happy Mother’s Day to All of the Mothers in Their Hearts!

# [Happy Mother’s Day to All of the Mothers in Their Hearts!](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/2026/05/10/happy-mothers-day-to-all-of-the-mothers-in-their-hearts/?jetpack_skip_subscription_popup)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on May 10, 2026
---
| **District Round-up 05.10.26**
---
Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here:**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-farnham-for-congress-1](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=560234031e9041542e1f6634f6b54672ba1ed728cb9c6ff4bf7420433e04d148&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=36e22053b121ed81424e1675661dc816&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b)**
---
| | |
---
4 generations – my maternal grandmother, her mother, my great grandmother, my exasperated mother, and Yours, Truly.
---
**Happy Mother’s Day**
to all of the mothers in their hearts.
---

Sometimes, it doesn’t go perfectly.
---

But every woman (including mothers) deserves the right to vote, to make decisions about her own body, to open independent financial accounts, to be paid equally for equal work, to own property, to be considered equally human.
---

While I find my family photo amusing, I also realize the pivotal changes these women supported. They were registered Democrats, not the die-hard kind, but those who lived out the equality others fought so hard for.
---
My great grandmother was born in 1906, so she saw her mother and older sisters vote for the first time in Georgia in 1922, due to registration rules.

My grandmother opened her own business in the 1980’s, holding financial accounts in her name only.
---

My mother divorced my father and had to pay child support because she simply chose not to seek custody of us.
---

And I was born the month after Roe v. Wade was decided 7-2 in favor of women’s bodily autonomy, only to see it overturned in June 2022.
---

Now, like so many mothers, I have to worry that my own daughter could die of sepsis if she: moves to a state where abortion is pretty much outlawed and miscarries. I have to worry that the Save America Act (see Mercersburg Springfest post below) will pass the Senate and inhibit our rights to vote.
---
But I keep on fighting. It’s why you see pink on my signs and in my logo. I will never forsake our causes. And pink was my mother’s favorite color, so it’s a nice reminder of her.
---
I choose Women’s Equality, I Choose Democracy and I hope you do too.
---
| |
---
| **Signs, Signs, Everywhere Signs!**
---
On Monday evening, Team Beth Farnham for Congress assembled 900 yard signs and loaded them onto a delivery truck. On Tuesday and Wednesday, I drove the truck, accompanied by Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, around the district to deliver those signs to Democratic Committee chairs, as well as put up the 8ft signs in the yards of our supporters.
---
| |
---
Pictured: Brian Ecker, Jack Stansbury, Barb Fisher, Joe Wagner, Bretni Lentz, Beth Farnham, Kelly Griswold, Sandy Hartzell, and Deb Martinez.
---
| |
8ft sign at Adams County Dem Headquarters.
---|---
Many, many thanks to Jack for his very hard work in putting them up – he did the lion’s share of the rebar hammering – and to the wonderful people also made this possible – Garrett Blanchet who made the frames, Danajoy Monroe, Matthew Lane, Farmer Rachel, Betsy Mason, and Dr. Bill Freeman for providing the settings, and the Democratic Committee chairs who accepted the hundreds of yard signs to be placed at the polls for the Primary Election on May 19th.
Even if Republicans don’t vote for me in that election, they will be familiar with my name so that when Trump crosses a line they didn’t know they had, they will have the confidence to vote for me in the General Election because my name has been a steady alternative to our current congressperson who only supports Trump.
---
| |
---
Jack worked very hard to install the sign on Betsy Mason’s property which sits on Route 22 between Altoona and Huntingdon.
---
|
Signs with the Mifflin Dems. |
Sign on Freeman property across from Marriott entrance/exit.|
---|---|---
|
Danajoy Monroe with the sign on her yard near the Gallitzen voting precinct.|
Yard Signs at Cambria Dems headquarters.
---|---|---

Matthew Lane with the sign affixed to his house.|
The sign on Farmer Rachel’s barn.|
Jack working very hard to get things just right.
---|---|---

Garrett Blanchet who built and lent to us the wonderful frames.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=562d9bef78041d4976186903016d6b5148632e437ebe59c479148b795b53a219&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=983de55e1093abb77e062d9fdda4c725&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b) !
---
| | **Endorsements Alert!**
---
Many thanks to the Progressive Voters Network for their endorsement!
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=49d57de3a667f389ff87731e36465ac3cd3ad6be8579b52c67b71de6da413840&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=c00b51c7549be4e80107178c1639e536&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b) !
---
| |
---
Their statement reads:
---
Why Beth
---
Beth Farnham stands as a true fighter for the working class and oppressed in PA-13, refusing to compromise with corporate Democrats or the billionaire class. She recognizes that real change demands dismantling systems of exploitation, not tweaking them. As an everyday activist turned candidate, Beth has marched for Black Lives Matter and demanded agency over our bodies through full reproductive freedom, including unapologetic abortion access without restrictions. Her vision rejects the status quo of profit-driven healthcare and instead fights for Medicare for All because no one should die or go bankrupt while the rich hoard wealth.
---
Beth’s commitment to immigration justice energizes those who understand borders as tools of white supremacy and capitalist control. She calls for abolishing ICE, ending the cruel machinery of deportation and family separation that terrorizes our communities. Clear paths to citizenship for all aren’t just policy. They’re reparations for decades of imperialist plunder. Beth will confront the fascist border regime head-on, standing with migrants as essential workers who built this country, not criminals to be hunted. This unyielding stance against carceral solutions separates her from milquetoast liberals who fear truly transformative action.
---
Beth demands a living wage, universal healthcare and raising taxes to fund the people’s needs, directly challenging the capitalist order. She backs union power without apology and recognizes that public education must serve as a tool for liberation, not standardized testing factories that reproduce inequality. Her pledge to fight affordability crises by confronting corporate greed resonates with everyone tired of scraping by while billionaires buy politicians. Beth knows climate change demands radical action now.
---
Beth Farnham’s record includes bold calls to impeach those undermining democracy and to prioritize people over profits. She has spoken truths about gun violence as a public health epidemic rooted in a sick society, pushing for real safety measures that go beyond thoughts and prayers. For those building power from below, Beth represents the kind of representative who will amplify movements, not co-opt or silence them, voting to check executive overreach and corporate capture at every turn.
---
In this moment of crisis, voting for Beth Farnham is a declaration that another world is possible. One free from war, policing of the poor and environmental destruction for profit. She turns activism into governance, carrying forward the struggles of unions, civil rights fighters and everyday resisters. PA-13 needs her uncompromising voice in Congress to push the boundaries leftward and deliver for the many, not the few.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=5a7273d028b835ab0242c305e48547532213d24bde3a62f6ba270f5103e0e791&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=b9affa9abd9ca09c6f7a9d894b15424d&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b)
---
| **Mercersburg** **Springfest**
---
As I approached the gentleman walking by my Beth Farnham for Congress campaign tent with my request for him to vote for me, he paused and asked sharply, “What party?”
---
“I am a registered Democrat,” I proudly told him.
---
“Nah, no Democrat” he answered with a dismissive wave of his hand and continued his walk.
---
To his retreating back, I said, “I celebrate your freedom to choose.”
---
An hour later he was back at my tent with a question.
---
“Do you support open borders?”
---
“No,” I responded simply.
---
Perhaps he expected a big fight or some kind of talking point, but whatever conservative media told him I’d do didn’t happen and he was stupified for a moment.
---
Finally, he asked, “What about the illegals who get all that money? Especially in the sanctuary cities?”
---
I told him, “Undocumented immigrants might receive money from charities, but they are not receiving bunches of money from our government. They are here because American employers hired them, from whom they received an Individual Taxpayer Identification Number, then they pay federal, state, local, and sales taxes. We rely on them for their cheap labor and the taxes they pay, especially in Franklin and Adams county where they pick our fruit and contribute to our economy. Those who overstay their visas are then considered, “undocumented” but they continue to do work and pay their taxes.”
---
“Yeah, and then they vote,” he responded.
---
I replied, “Non-citizens do not vote and there isn’t massive fraud as the Republicans are claiming, but the Save America Act is going to infringe on the rights of many married American women.”
---
“Well, not if you have a real I.D.” he retorted.
---
“Unfortunately, the state of Pennsylvania is not one of the states where a Real I.D. will not count as proof of citizenship for people whose names don’t match those on their birth certificates, mostly married women,” I answered.
---
“But you can use a Real I.D. to fly around the United States. My wife has one of those,” he asserted.
---
“Yes, but again, Pennsylvania is not one of the five states where the Real I.D. can count as proof of citizenship. Your wife will need a current passport to prove her citizenship when she re-registers to vote due to a change of address, political party, name, or if she is purged from the voting rolls for not voting often enough,” I explained.
---
“Yeah, well, she has a current passport,” he said.
---
“Great,” I told him, “As long as she maintains a current passport for the rest of her life, renewing it as necessary, she won’t have to worry about proving her citizenship to register to vote with the state of Pennsylvania anytime she moves, etc.,” I let him know, but the truth was dawning on his face as he began to realize the older she got, the more challenging that situation was going to be.
---
“You see,” I went further, “If a woman needs to spend $200 on obtaining a passport just to prove her citizenship to change her address as a voter with a state that already was satisfied when she first registered to vote with her social security card, driver’s license, and marriage certificate long ago, it’s an undue burden like a poll tax on that woman and everyone like her whose name doesn’t match that on her birth certificate.”
---
“Yeah, well every state should have the Real I.D. that counts,” he added.
---
“States’ rights,” I quipped and he blinked.
---
“You know, my best friend and I are the only survivors of our unit that went to Vietnam. [I thanked him for his service.] But we don’t agree on politics so he never talks to me about it. I just want to have a conversation, but he won’t open up. You talked to me though,” he mused.
---
“It’s my pleasure. Please vote for me,” I said.
---
And he walked away, very, very quietly.
---
This is why I put my tent up in the deepest, reddest parts of our 13th Congressional District. I know there are a lot of voters out there who have not been given information they deserve to know.
---
Many thanks to my volunteers – Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, Deb Martinez, Claire Meissner, Sue Oberman, Angie Sulman, and Chris for their tremendous help at the Mercersburg Springfest to make this moment possible.
---
May that veteran #ChooseDemocracy.
---
(Photo credits: Jack Stansbury and Sue Oberman, gentleman in story not pictured)
---

| **Radio Interview**
---
Many thanks to Lloyd Roach of Bedford County Speaks Radio Program for interviewing me ahead of the May 19th Primary Election. Lloyd asked great questions and uploaded it to the Bedford Speaks website.
---
| [](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=aba9a0a7814704bdcaa17fcd1d8218e8aeca9e53cb7de7ab1dd2d14fccf72983&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=890d6cb673f5d68e60ada789fd391a00&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b)| [ Listen to the audio ](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=aba9a0a7814704bdcaa17fcd1d8218e8aeca9e53cb7de7ab1dd2d14fccf72983&blog_id=213069660&post_id=7305&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=890d6cb673f5d68e60ada789fd391a00&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=96e9bf70e246f0ab98a2b54a62a9d20b)

Please listen to it, then vote for me in the May 19th primary.
---
If you’re a registered Democrat, I’m on your ballot to vote for.
---

clean_body: District Round-up 05.10.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you... | | | | | or ---|---|--- By Elizabeth Farnham on May 10, 2026 | District Round-up 05.10.26 Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: | | | 4 generations – my maternal grandmother, her mother, my great grandmother, my exasperated mother, and Yours, Truly. Happy Mother’s Day to all of the mothers in their hearts. Sometimes, it doesn’t go perfectly. But every woman (including mothers) deserves the right to vote, to make decisions about her own body, to open independent financial accounts, to be paid equally for equal work, to own property, to be considered equally human. While I find my family photo amusing, I also realize the pivotal changes these women supported. They were registered Democrats, not the die-hard kind, but those who lived out the equality others fought so hard for. My great grandmother was born in 1906, so she saw her mother and older sisters vote for the first time in Georgia in 1922, due to registration rules. My grandmother opened her own business in the 1980’s, holding financial accounts in her name only. My mother divorced my father and had to pay child support because she simply chose not to seek custody of us. And I was born the month after Roe v. Wade was decided 7-2 in favor of women’s bodily autonomy, only to see it overturned in June 2022. Now, like so many mothers, I have to worry that my own daughter could die of sepsis if she: moves to a state where abortion is pretty much outlawed and miscarries. I have to worry that the Save America Act (see Mercersburg Springfest post below) will pass the Senate and inhibit our rights to vote. But I keep on fighting. It’s why you see pink on my signs and in my logo. I will never forsake our causes. And pink was my mother’s favorite color, so it’s a nice reminder of her. I choose Women’s Equality, I Choose Democracy and I hope you do too. | | | Signs, Signs, Everywhere Signs! On Monday evening, Team Beth Farnham for Congress assembled 900 yard signs and loaded them onto a delivery truck. On Tuesday and Wednesday, I drove the truck, accompanied by Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, around the district to deliver those signs to Democratic Committee chairs, as well as put up the 8ft signs in the yards of our supporters. | | Pictured: Brian Ecker, Jack Stansbury, Barb Fisher, Joe Wagner, Bretni Lentz, Beth Farnham, Kelly Griswold, Sandy Hartzell, and Deb Martinez. | | 8ft sign at Adams County Dem Headquarters. ---|--- Many, many thanks to Jack for his very hard work in putting them up – he did the lion’s share of the rebar hammering – and to the wonderful people also made this possible – Garrett Blanchet who made the frames, Danajoy Monroe, Matthew Lane, Farmer Rachel, Betsy Mason, and Dr. Bill Freeman for providing the settings, and the Democratic Committee chairs who accepted the hundreds of yard signs to be placed at the polls for the Primary Election on May 19th. Even if Republicans don’t vote for me in that election, they will be familiar with my name so that when Trump crosses a line they didn’t know they had, they will have the confidence to vote for me in the General Election because my name has been a steady alternative to our current congressperson who only supports Trump. | | Jack worked very hard to install the sign on Betsy Mason’s property which sits on Route 22 between Altoona and Huntingdon. | Signs with the Mifflin Dems. | Sign on Freeman property across from Marriott entrance/exit.| ---|---|--- | Danajoy Monroe with the sign on her yard near the Gallitzen voting precinct.| Yard Signs at Cambria Dems headquarters. ---|---|--- Matthew Lane with the sign affixed to his house.| The sign on Farmer Rachel’s barn.| Jack working very hard to get things just right. ---|---|--- Garrett Blanchet who built and lent to us the wonderful frames. Together, we ! | | Endorsements Alert! Many thanks to the Progressive Voters Network for their endorsement! Together, we ! | | Their statement reads: Why Beth Beth Farnham stands as a true fighter for the working class and oppressed in PA-13, refusing to compromise with corporate Democrats or the billionaire class. She recognizes that real change demands dismantling systems of exploitation, not tweaking them. As an everyday activist turned candidate, Beth has marched for Black Lives Matter and demanded agency over our bodies through full reproductive freedom, including unapologetic abortion access without restrictions. Her vision rejects the status quo of profit-driven healthcare and instead fights for Medicare for All because no one should die or go bankrupt while the rich hoard wealth. Beth’s commitment to immigration justice energizes those who understand borders as tools of white supremacy and capitalist control. She calls for abolishing ICE, ending the cruel machinery of deportation and family separation that terrorizes our communities. Clear paths to citizenship for all aren’t just policy. They’re reparations for decades of imperialist plunder. Beth will confront the fascist border regime head-on, standing with migrants as essential workers who built this country, not criminals to be hunted. This unyielding stance against carceral solutions separates her from milquetoast liberals who fear truly transformative action. Beth demands a living wage, universal healthcare and raising taxes to fund the people’s needs, directly challenging the capitalist order. She backs union power without apology and recognizes that public education must serve as a tool for liberation, not standardized testing factories that reproduce inequality. Her pledge to fight affordability crises by confronting corporate greed resonates with everyone tired of scraping by while billionaires buy politicians. Beth knows climate change demands radical action now. Beth Farnham’s record includes bold calls to impeach those undermining democracy and to prioritize people over profits. She has spoken truths about gun violence as a public health epidemic rooted in a sick society, pushing for real safety measures that go beyond thoughts and prayers. For those building power from below, Beth represents the kind of representative who will amplify movements, not co-opt or silence them, voting to check executive overreach and corporate capture at every turn. In this moment of crisis, voting for Beth Farnham is a declaration that another world is possible. One free from war, policing of the poor and environmental destruction for profit. She turns activism into governance, carrying forward the struggles of unions, civil rights fighters and everyday resisters. PA-13 needs her uncompromising voice in Congress to push the boundaries leftward and deliver for the many, not the few. Together, we | Mercersburg Springfest As I approached the gentleman walking by my Beth Farnham for Congress campaign tent with my request for him to vote for me, he paused and asked sharply, “What party?” “I am a registered Democrat,” I proudly told him. “Nah, no Democrat” he answered with a dismissive wave of his hand and continued his walk. To his retreating back, I said, “I celebrate your freedom to choose.” An hour later he was back at my tent with a question. “Do you support open borders?” “No,” I responded simply. Perhaps he expected a big fight or some kind of talking point, but whatever conservative media told him I’d do didn’t happen and he was stupified for a moment. Finally, he asked, “What about the illegals who get all that money? Especially in the sanctuary cities?” I told him, “Undocumented immigrants might receive money from charities, but they are not receiving bunches of money from our government. They are here because American employers hired them, from whom they received an Individual Taxpayer Identification Number, then they pay federal, state, local, and sales taxes. We rely on them for their cheap labor and the taxes they pay, especially in Franklin and Adams county where they pick our fruit and contribute to our economy. Those who overstay their visas are then considered, “undocumented” but they continue to do work and pay their taxes.” “Yeah, and then they vote,” he responded. I replied, “Non-citizens do not vote and there isn’t massive fraud as the Republicans are claiming, but the Save America Act is going to infringe on the rights of many married American women.” “Well, not if you have a real I.D.” he retorted. “Unfortunately, the state of Pennsylvania is not one of the states where a Real I.D. will not count as proof of citizenship for people whose names don’t match those on their birth certificates, mostly married women,” I answered. “But you can use a Real I.D. to fly around the United States. My wife has one of those,” he asserted. “Yes, but again, Pennsylvania is not one of the five states where the Real I.D. can count as proof of citizenship. Your wife will need a current passport to prove her citizenship when she re-registers to vote due to a change of address, political party, name, or if she is purged from the voting rolls for not voting often enough,” I explained. “Yeah, well, she has a current passport,” he said. “Great,” I told him, “As long as she maintains a current passport for the rest of her life, renewing it as necessary, she won’t have to worry about proving her citizenship to register to vote with the state of Pennsylvania anytime she moves, etc.,” I let him know, but the truth was dawning on his face as he began to realize the older she got, the more challenging that situation was going to be. “You see,” I went further, “If a woman needs to spend $200 on obtaining a passport just to prove her citizenship to change her address as a voter with a state that already was satisfied when she first registered to vote with her social security card, driver’s license, and marriage certificate long ago, it’s an undue burden like a poll tax on that woman and everyone like her whose name doesn’t match that on her birth certificate.” “Yeah, well every state should have the Real I.D. that counts,” he added. “States’ rights,” I quipped and he blinked. “You know, my best friend and I are the only survivors of our unit that went to Vietnam. [I thanked him for his service.] But we don’t agree on politics so he never talks to me about it. I just want to have a conversation, but he won’t open up. You talked to me though,” he mused. “It’s my pleasure. Please vote for me,” I said. And he walked away, very, very quietly. This is why I put my tent up in the deepest, reddest parts of our 13th Congressional District. I know there are a lot of voters out there who have not been given information they deserve to know. Many thanks to my volunteers – Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, Deb Martinez, Claire Meissner, Sue Oberman, Angie Sulman, and Chris for their tremendous help at the Mercersburg Springfest to make this moment possible. May that veteran #ChooseDemocracy. (Photo credits: Jack Stansbury and Sue Oberman, gentleman in story not pictured) | | | | | ---|---|--- | ---|--- | Radio Interview Many thanks to Lloyd Roach of Bedford County Speaks Radio Program for interviewing me ahead of the May 19th Primary Election. Lloyd asked great questions and uploaded it to the Bedford Speaks website. | | ---|--- Please listen to it, then vote for me in the May 19th primary. If you’re a registered Democrat, I’m on your ballot to vote for. If you’re a registered Republican, please write in my name, Beth Farnham, then darken the corresponding oval, instead of choosing John Joyce. Together, we ! | Charity of the Week! In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. clean_body: You need to see this. | Patriot, Direct from the White House, President Trump brought national attention to a disloyal Republican Senator who is attacking the agenda YOU voted for. First, Senator Bill Cassidy voted to CONVICT President Trump in the sham impeachment trial in the Senate. Then, Cassidy chose to make enemies with RFK Jr. and OBSTRUCT the MAHA agenda. Now, Cassidy has been CAUGHT red-handed telling Democrat voters to INFILTRATE the Republican primary election to vote for him and stop the Trump-endorsed candidate from winning. Cassidy knows he cannot win a fair election against Trump-endorsed Congresswoman Julia Letlow who’s SURGING in the polls, so he’s resorting to dirty tactics to remain in power. To top it all off, Cassidy just dropped $12 MILLION worth of attack ads against Julia Letlow with only DAYS to go until our blockbuster primary election! With $12 MILLION at his disposal, Cassidy believes he can recruit enough Democrat voters into the GOP primary to win... ...But he’s in for a rude awakening. Because our campaign has what this RINO NEVER will: a grassroots movement of millions of MAGA patriots nationwide ready to fight back against his lies and smears. Just like with the President’s campaign, it’s not about the amount our supporters give, it’s about the sheer number of patriots standing with President Trump and me. Cassidy may have $12 million worth of attack ads, but we have millions of patriots giving $25, $50, even $100 at a time to FIRE him once and for all. - Paid for by Julia Letlow for Louisiana P. O. Box 106 Baton Rouge, LA 70821
